description: README.md:11-13 and src/lib.rs:3 advertise the URI shape 'secretx:wolfhsm:<label>[?server=<addr>][?client_id=<n>]' — two ? markers. SecretUri::parse (secretx-core/src/lib.rs:1135) splits on the FIRST '?' and treats the rest as a single query string parsed on '&'. Trigger: a user copies the documented example and writes 'secretx:wolfhsm:foo?server=127.0.0.1:8080?client_id=2' — the parser produces params { server: '127.0.0.1:8080?client_id=2' } and from_uri() then errors out with 'port 8080?client_id=2 is not a valid u16'. The user reads the docs, follows them, gets a confusing error. Fix: change README and rustdoc to '?server=<addr>&client_id=<n>' (single ?, & separator), matching how every other secretx crate documents URIs.
title: URI grammar in README and rustdoc shows ?server=...?client_id=... but parser only honors single ?
